canonical_definition: A brain alignment measurement procedure where a linear regression model is trained to predict brain activity from model representations, with performance measured by the Pearson correlation on held-out data.
papers: Mind the Blind Spots: A Focus-Level Evaluation Framework forLLMReviews
Sketch-of-Thought: EfficientLLMReasoning with Adaptive Cognitive-Inspired Sketching
